water.name: Guadalupe River
aliases: Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(47) — Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir (Santa Clara Co.)
sources.quotedText: (47) Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir (Santa Clara Co.) including Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake, and Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures and barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: D4: Fish Habitat and Passage Improvement — Santa Clara Valley Water
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 253220, "Guadalupe River", Stream, CA, Santa Clara Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":253220,"gaz_name":"Guadalupe River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Santa Clara","objectid_1":1821824,"isunknowncoords":2}, southernmost vertex of the named stream, near its source at the Guadalupe Creek/Alamitos Creek confluence by Almaden Lake (Web Mercator -13566837.3039, 4473146.1536 → 37.24381°N, 121.87297°W). Corrects prior file coordinates (-121.8720, 37.2050), which were placed at Guadalupe Reservoir itself (roughly 4.3 km south, on Guadalupe Creek upstream of the confluence that forms the Guadalupe River) rather than on the Guadalupe River; the corrected point is corroborated by Almaden Lake Park's published coordinates (37.24203°N, 121.87410°W), immediately adjacent to that confluence.

groups.humanSummary: Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir (Santa Clara Co.), including Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake, and Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir: open last Saturday in April through November 15, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (47) Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir (Santa Clara Co.) including Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake, and Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures and barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.description: This entry covers the Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir dam and also applies identically to Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake dam, and to Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir dam, all in Santa Clara County.

included_waters: Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake

regulations.humanSummary: Regulation covers Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir, and also applies to Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake, and Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: Guadalupe River below Guadalupe Reservoir (Santa Clara Co.) including Los Gatos Creek below Vasona Lake, and Alamitos Creek and Arroyo Calero below Calero Reservoir.

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through November 15.
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15.

parameters.note: Applies throughout the open season, last Saturday in April through November 15.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures and barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures and barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
